WORK PERFORMED:
  EXP-272 c1 follow-up to EXP-254 §4.2.7 (eclipse trilemma). Produced two coordinated patches:

  P1 (data/sections.json part4 §4.2.7 prose, ISS-1608 H3 fix): the integrated parenthetical "...a more central orbit yields ~25 km/min, which lengthens penumbra crossing to a few minutes but leaves the 1.3-km totality-circle diameter unchanged" contains a math error — totality DIAMETER is unchanged but per-observer totality DURATION scales as 1.3/v and DOES change. Replacement parenthetical correctly propagates 0.9 sec @ 87 km/min (rim-orbit upper bound) and ~3 sec @ 25 km/min (mid-disc, Aug 2026 path latitude). Added 45× shortfall robustness sentence and angular-velocity cross-check (~2°/min at zenith for rim orbit, ~0.5°/min for mid-disc) per ISS-1608's unit-system request. Verified math: 1.3/87×60=0.90 sec, 1.3/25×60=3.12 sec, 138.2/3.12=44.3× (rounded to 45×), 87/2534 rad/min × 180/π = 1.97°/min. Standard Moon apparent angular ~0.25°/min (Earth rotation dominant) — so dome zenith speed is ~8× faster, not absurd but firmly not the standard Moon either.

  P2 (docs/assets/eclipse-geometry-comparison.svg annotation panel, ISS-1607 H2 cleanup): integrated SVG had three bullets and a redundant red-text patch line "~1.3 km track (≈ 1/220 of observed path)" that duplicated the first bullet. Three coordinated edits: (a) first bullet "1.3 km totality circle" → "1.3 km totality track" (addresses ISS-1607's static-framing concern); (b) ratio gains "294 km" for clarity; (c) redundant red line replaced with "Track moves; per-observer ~0.9 sec" — explicitly closes the "spot moves with the Sun" rebuttal: yes, the track moves, but per-observer dwell is still ~0.9 sec.

  Math verification ran in Python: rim_orbit=87.33 km/min ✓, 64°N flat-earth-Mercator radius gives 25 km/min ✓, totality at one observer at v=25 = 3.12 sec ✓, angular at zenith for v=87,h=2534 = 1.97°/min ✓, robustness ratio 138.2/3.12 = 44.3× ✓.
